text: SHTULA CEASEFIRE BREACH: Hezbollah fired 4 rockets at Shtula border community ~11:30 PM local (April 23) — FIRST rocket barrage since ceasefire. All intercepted. Hezbollah: 'In response to Israeli violations and targeting of Yater.' IDF retaliated within minutes hitting launchers in Yater and Kafra. Evacuation order for Deir Aames issued. 30+ Hezbollah operatives killed since ceasefire per IDF.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Ya Libnan, Jerusalem Post, Israel National News. All verified.

text: BINT JBEIL FIREFIGHT: IDF Paratroopers Brigade sent Oketz K9 unit dog into building in Bint Jbeil — ambushed by 6 Hezbollah gunmen who killed the dog. Within 90 minutes IDF killed all 6 using light arms, tank shelling, and explosive drone. No IDF casualties. IDF cumulative: 30+ Hezbollah operatives killed since ceasefire, hundreds of sites destroyed.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed.

text: TRUMP RULES OUT NUKES: 'A nuclear weapon should never be allowed to be used by anybody. Why would I use a nuclear weapon? We've totally, in a very conventional way, decimated them.' Also told Reuters Iran plans to 'make an offer.' Witkoff/Kushner depart Saturday for Islamabad Round 2. Araghchi lands in Islamabad; Iran denies talks planned. IRGC: 'eye for head' — 'fingers on trigger.'
verificationNote: PBS, Washington Times, US News, Dawn, PressTV. All verified.

text: OIL FRIDAY: Brent $104.50 (-0.54% on session), WTI $94.65 — pullback on late signal Iran says Hormuz 'completely open' to commercial shipping ahead of possible Pakistan talks deal. Both on track for ~15% weekly gain. Gas $4.03 AAA (-6¢ WoW). CENTCOM: 34 ships now redirected (up from 31). Blockade Day 54. IMF: global growth cut to 3.1%.
verificationNote: CNBC, The National, AAA Newsroom, PBS IMF. All verified.

text: NETANYAHU: Accuses Hezbollah of trying to 'sabotage historic peace' with Lebanon. Had 'excellent conversation' with Trump. 'We maintain full freedom of action.' Hezbollah MP Fayyad: ceasefire 'meaningless.' Katz: Israel 'ready to resume war' — awaiting US green light to 'return Iran to Stone Age.' IRGC generals effectively ruling Iran; Mojtaba Khamenei sidelined.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Haaretz, Washington Examiner, Euronews, Jerusalem Post. All verified.

text: DAY 57 PM ROUNDUP: Brent $104.50 (+30.9%), WTI $94.65 (+25.4%). Hezbollah fires 4 rockets at Shtula — first barrage since ceasefire. IDF kills 6 in Bint Jbeil. Hezbollah targets APC in Ramyah. 6 killed in south Lebanon. Trump: no nukes, Iran to 'make an offer.' Araghchi in Islamabad; Iran denies talks. IRGC 'eye for head.' Netanyahu: Hezbollah 'sabotaging peace.' Gas $4.03. Blockade Day 54.
verificationNote: CNBC, ToI, PBS, Dawn, PressTV, AAA. All verified.

text: SANCTIONS BLITZ: OFAC froze $344M in USDT on Tron blockchain linked to IRGC/Hezbollah — largest crypto enforcement of the war. Tether cooperated. Separately, Treasury sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical (Dalian) — China's biggest independent refinery (400K bpd), Iran's largest crude customer — plus 40 shipping entities. Bessent: 'We will follow the money.'
verificationNote: CNN, Bloomberg Law, Treasury.gov (sb0472), Washington Times. All verified.

text: BESSENT BOMBSHELL: Treasury Secretary says US will NOT renew waivers for Iranian OR Russian oil. 'Not the Iranians. We have the blockade, there's no oil coming out. In 2-3 days they're going to have to start shuttering production — very bad for their wells.' Baker Hughes CEO: Hormuz 'may not fully reopen until second half of 2026.'
verificationNote: Washington Times, ABC News, CNBC (Baker Hughes earnings call). All verified.

text: BLOCKADE UPDATE: USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) intercepted and boarded Iranian-flagged ship in Strait of Hormuz April 24 — CENTCOM released photos. Total 34 vessels redirected since April 13. 3 tankers seized. 3 carriers in theater: Ford (Red Sea), Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Bush (Indian Ocean). Combined: 12 warships, 200+ aircraft, 15,000 troops.
verificationNote: CGTN, WION (CENTCOM photo release), USNI News, Stars and Stripes. All verified.

text: IRAN DE-ESCALATION: Tehran resumed international flights from IKIA after 50+ day hiatus — first flights to Istanbul, Muscat, Beijing. 6 airports reopened. Major signal ahead of Islamabad Round 2. Meanwhile Soufan Center reports Iran's information warfare campaign has been 'surprisingly effective' — AI-generated content, disinformation, influence campaigns.
verificationNote: PressTV, Middle East Monitor, WION (flights). Soufan Center IntelBrief April 24 (info warfare).

text: MUNITIONS CRISIS: CSIS reports US has expended $24B in major munitions in 7 weeks. 45% of Precision Strike Missiles used. 50% of THAAD interceptors depleted. ~50% of Patriot interceptors fired (1,060-1,430 of 2,330). 1,000+ JASSM-ER and 1,000+ Tomahawks expended. Restocking timeline: 1-4 YEARS to return to pre-war levels. Pentagon FY27 request: $1.5T (war costs NOT included).
verificationNote: Fortune (munitions depleted), Fox News (Patriot), CSIS analysis April 24. All verified.

text: DAY 57 LATE ROUNDUP: Brent settles $105.33 (+31.9%), WTI $94.40 (+25.0%) — best week since March. OFAC $344M crypto freeze. Hengli + 40 ships sanctioned. Bessent: no oil waivers, Iran shuttering in days. Iran resumes flights. CSIS: $24B munitions, 50% Patriot used. Baker Hughes: Hormuz not open til H2. S&P 7,165 (record). Gas $4.031.
verificationNote: CNBC, CNN, Treasury.gov, WashTimes, ABC News, PressTV, Fortune, CSIS, Motley Fool, AAA. All verified.

text: NAVAL UPDATE: USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) intercepted Iranian-flagged vessel — CENTCOM now at 34 redirections since April 13 blockade. US-sanctioned supertanker 'Yuri' carrying 2M barrels of Iranian crude from Kharg halted near Larak Island — deterred by blockade. 3 carriers in theater: Ford (Red Sea), Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Bush (Indian Ocean). First 3-carrier Middle East deployment since 2003.
verificationNote: ANI News, Xinhua, CENTCOM photos. Bloomberg/World Oil (Yuri). Breaking Defense (3 carriers).

text: CYBER FRONT OPENS: Joint CISA/FBI/NSA advisory confirms Iranian APT exploiting PLCs across US water, energy, and government sectors since March. Pro-Iran Ababil of Minab claimed LA Metro hack. This is the first systematic Iranian cyber campaign against US homeland infrastructure since the war began. 60+ hacktivist groups now active.
verificationNote: CISA advisory AA26-097a. Defense One confirmed. LA Metro hack from multiple sources.

text: ISLAMABAD ROUND 2 STALLING: Araghchi meeting Pakistani PM Sharif, Army Chief Munir, FM Dar but EXPLICITLY ruling out any direct talks with US. Iran FM spokesperson: 'No meeting planned with US.' Iran Defense Ministry: US 'looking for face-saving exit.' Witkoff/Kushner departure reportedly pushed to Sunday. Gap between sides widening, not narrowing.
verificationNote: Euronews, Al Jazeera liveblog, CNBC, NPR, Times of Israel liveblog. All confirmed.

text: IRAN ECONOMY COLLAPSING: IMF projects -6.1% GDP contraction, 68.9% inflation, 2M jobs lost since February. Bessent: Iran must 'shutter production in 2-3 days' as Kharg Island storage nears capacity. $344M crypto frozen. Hengli (China's biggest Iran customer) sanctioned. Combined all-countries war cost: $177B. Harvard: 'certain' to hit $1T over decade.
verificationNote: CNBC (Iran economy charts), The National (IMF data), CNN (crypto), Treasury.gov (Hengli), CNBC (Harvard $1T).

text: AI DISINFORMATION CRISIS: NewsGuard tracked fabricated war visuals with 21.9M+ views on X alone. Pro-Iran AI video of 'Iranian plane confronting US ship' got 7M+ views. AI verification tools mislabeling real videos as fake AND fake as real. Called 'the first AI war.' Meanwhile Mojtaba Khamenei still unseen — ruling via handwritten notes passed by motorcycle couriers per NYT.
verificationNote: Poynter (AI disinformation), Israel Hayom/NYT (Mojtaba injuries), TIME (power structure).

text: DAY 57 AM REFRESH: USS Rafael Peralta intercepts Iranian vessel — 34 redirected. Supertanker Yuri (2M bbl) halts at Larak. CISA: Iranian APT hitting US water/energy. Araghchi in Islamabad — no direct US talks. Witkoff/Kushner delayed to Sunday. Hegseth: NATO 'free-riding over.' Gold $4,710-4,741. Brent $105.33. S&P 7,165 record. Gas $4.031.
verificationNote: ANI/CENTCOM, Bloomberg, CISA, Euronews, Washington Times, Sunday Guardian, AAA. All verified.

text: TRUMP CANCELS PAKISTAN TRIP: Truth Social — 'I just cancelled the trip of my representatives going to Islamabad. We have all the cards, they have none. If they want to talk, all they have to do is call!' Reverses Karoline Leavitt's Friday Fox News announcement. Iran subsequently sent 'much better' paper but 'still not adequate.' Marks second collapse of Islamabad track after April 12.
verificationNote: CBS News, NPR, Times of Israel liveblog. All verified — direct Truth Social quote.

text: LEBANON CEASEFIRE BREACH: Hezbollah fires 2 rockets + multiple drones at northern Israel — sirens at Manara, Margaliot, Misgav Am. First rocket barrage since April 16 ceasefire. Drone triggers Malkia sirens, IDF loses contact (UAV crashed). Netanyahu's office orders IDF to 'vigorously strike' Hezbollah. IDF targets Radwan Force facilities + locates anti-tank missile cache. Haaretz tally: 15+ killed in Lebanon since ceasefire began.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Haaretz, Al Jazeera. All verified.

text: IRAN POWER STRUGGLE: ISW assesses IRGC Cmdr Maj. Gen. Ahmad Vahidi has 'upper hand' over Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf in intra-regime power struggle. Vahidi may be deliberately introducing preconditions to derail US talks. IRGC Navy's redirection of two vessels in Hormuz (April 22) reads as enforcement of sovereignty claims and signal Vahidi prepared to resume war.
verificationNote: Critical Threats Project — Iran Update April 22, 2026 Special Report.

text: IRAN AUTHORITY VACUUM: Pragmatic Iranian leaders argue resumption of hostilities would be catastrophic, while IRGC hardliners view compromise with Washington as 'sellout of Iran's Islamic revolution.' Soufan: 'There is no clearly identifiable overarching authority in Iran capable of resolving the internal debate.' Mojtaba Khamenei communicating only via handwritten notes by motorcycle courier.
verificationNote: Soufan Center IntelBrief April 24. NYT/Israel Hayom on Mojtaba.

text: POLICE INFRASTRUCTURE TRACKED: Bellingcat documents at least 30 strikes on Iranian police stations and 29 on command centers/HQs, plus 9 Basij sites, 3 special forces, 2 traffic, 2 diplomatic police compounds. Identified using Sentinel-1 SAR satellite data. Frames campaign as effort to 'make Iran ungovernable.' Adds new dimension to coalition strike target set beyond military/nuclear sites.
verificationNote: Bellingcat report April 24, 2026. Sentinel-1 SAR data verified.

text: MARKETS HOLDING: Brent ~$105.50 (+32.1%), WTI ~$94.50 (+25.1%) — modest gains continuing on Trump cancellation. Gas $4.03 AAA (flat WoW). VLCC charter rates ~$148K/day (+362% from $32K pre-war). Lloyd's war risk premium 3.85% of hull value (~77x pre-war). ~340 tankers rerouted via Cape of Good Hope adding 21 days transit. TEDPIX (Tehran) suspended since March 5.
verificationNote: CNBC, Lloyd's List, Baltic Exchange, Clarksons, Iran International. All verified.

text: DAY 57 PM ROUNDUP: Trump CANCELS Witkoff/Kushner Pakistan trip — 'we have all the cards.' Hezbollah fires 2 rockets + drones at northern Israel; Netanyahu orders IDF 'vigorously strike.' IDF strikes Radwan facilities, finds anti-tank cache. EU's Costa demands Hormuz reopen. Kallas: nuclear experts needed. ISW: Vahidi has 'upper hand' in Iran. Brent ~$105.50. Gas $4.03 AAA. Hormuz Day 54 closed.
verificationNote: CBS, NPR, Times of Israel, Haaretz, EU Council, The National, ISW. All verified.
